text.tspan: Alignment from preferences. Given pairs of (preferred, rejected) outputs,
train the model to favor preferred. Replaces the complex RLHF pipeline
(reward model + PPO) with simpler direct training. The 2023-2025 evolution
of model alignment.
Step 1: Train a separate REWARD MODEL on (preferred, rejected) pairs.
Step 2: Use PPO (reinforcement learning) to optimize the LLM to maximize reward.
Problems: unstable PPO training, separate reward model, expensive,
many hyperparameters, hard to reproduce.
Rafailov et al. - 'Your language model is secretly a reward model.'
Directly optimize the LLM on preferences with a simple classifier-style loss.
No separate reward model, no PPO, much simpler.
Matches or beats RLHF quality at fraction of complexity.
Became the default by 2024.
KTO (Kahneman-Tversky Optimization, Feb 2024):
Works on UNPAIRED data - just 'good' / 'bad' labels per example.
Easier data collection.
ORPO (Hong et al. Mar 2024):
Combines preference + SFT in one stage. No reference model needed.
IPO, SimPO, CPO: assorted refinements.
GRPO (DeepSeek): group-relative; behind R1's reasoning training.
Libraries: TRL (HuggingFace), Axolotl, LLaMA-Factory, Unsloth.
Used by: Llama 3.1+, Mistral, Qwen, Gemma 2+, DeepSeek-V3, etc.
Data sources: human preferences, AI-feedback (RLAIF),
rejection sampling from a strong model.
Cost: cheaper than RLHF; still expensive (multi-GPU training).
For most app developers: not your problem; the base model already
went through preference tuning.
